publishedAbstract: * The resident set size of a glusterfs process in a Gluster client is showing a high amount of memory consumed. This can be verified in the `ps auxwww` command output: USER PID %CPU %MEM VSZ RSS TTY STAT START TIME COMMAND root 12443 40.0 1.0 4009164 2783356? Ssl 2019 2391:00 /usr/sbin/glusterfs--volfile-server=server1--volfile-id=/vol01 /mnt/gluster-mount The sixth field corresponds to the resident set size (RSS). This value is expressed in KB, so the amount of memory consumed by the process posted above is approximately 2.7 GB. * How to reduce this high memory usage?
publishedTitle: Why Does the Gluster Client Process ( glusterfs ),  Consume so Much Memory in Gluster versions below 3.4.4?
